div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, both present as well as to come, Uhtred of Grubbit, and Simon his son and heir, send greeting. Know us to have given and by this our charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, for the souls of all our lords, and for our own souls, and of our fathers' and mothers' souls, and of all our ancestors' and successors' souls, and especially of Earl Henry's soul, that pat of the land in 'Elstaneshalche', which lies on the western side of the old river, towards the land of the same monks which they have of the feu of Whitton, to the east of their land, in free, and pure, and perpetual alms, that they may have it, and hold it, and possess it, freely and quitely, and honourably, just as freely, and quitely, and honourably as they hold any alms, moreover from all earthly service and custom, and secular exaction, and we and our heirs shall warrant to them the same land against all men, and we shall discharge towards the lord king, and all our lords in respect of forinsec service, and all earthly service for ever. We have made this donation to them for the sake of fraternity, and for prayers, and for the participation in all the benefits of the same church for ever. With these witnesses: Godard the chaplain; Adam the parson of Maxton; Robert of Barnoldby.
